# First-Time Setup

::: {.callout-important}
## Execution Context
All admin operations in this chapter must be executed from the **project root directory** (where the `Makefile` and `docker-compose.yml` reside).
:::

This repository uses environment variables to configure the local domain, database credentials, and the Jupyter password.

1. Create a `.env` file (Docker Compose reads `.env` by default):

   ```bash
   cp .env.default .env
    ```

2. Edit `.env` to match your setup:
   - Set `DOMAIN` to your local domain (e.g. `localhost` or a IP)
   - Set strong passwords for `POSTGRES_PASS`
   - Configure `JUPYTERHUB_ADMINS` **(with at least one admin username)**
   - Optionally add regular users to `JUPYTERHUB_USERS`

   **Required variables:**

   | Variable              | Required | Example                    | Description                                                   |
   | --------------------- | -------: | -------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- |
   | `DOMAIN`              |      Yes | `localhost`                | Hostname used to access the web endpoints (Jupyter/Explorer). |
   | `IMAGE_VERSION`       |      Yes | `20260211`                 | Version of the images to use.                                 |
   | `POSTGRES_HOSTNAME`   |      Yes | `postgres`                 | Hostname used to access the PostgreSQL database.              |
   | `POSTGRES_PORT`       |      Yes | `5432`                     | Port used to access the PostgreSQL database.                  |
   | `POSTGRES_DBNAME`     |      Yes | `opendatacube`             | PostgreSQL database name used by Open Data Cube.              |
   | `POSTGRES_USER`       |      Yes | `opendatacube`             | PostgreSQL user for the Open Data Cube database.              |
   | `POSTGRES_PASS`       |      Yes | `a-strong-password`        | PostgreSQL password for the Open Data Cube database.          |
   | `JUPYTERHUB_ADMINS`   |      Yes | `admin,bruno`              | Comma-separated list of JupyterHub admin usernames.           |
   | `JUPYTERHUB_USERS`    |       No | `guest,alice,bob`          | Comma-separated list of authorized non-admin usernames.       |
: Required environment variables {#tbl-env-vars}

3. First-time setup
- Using default parameters:

  ```bash
  make setup
  ```

- With a specific area/time (`BBOX`, `DATETIME`):
 
  ```bash
  # Switzerland 1 year
  make setup BBOX=5.95,45.81,10.50,47.81 DATETIME=2024-01-01/2024-12-31
  
  # Switzerland all years (till end 2025, might take a while)
  make setup BBOX=5.95,45.81,10.50,47.81 DATETIME=1984-01-01/2025-12-31
  ```

   | Parameter | Format | Description |
   | :--- | :--- | :--- |
   | **`BBOX`** | `lon_min,lat_min,lon_max,lat_max` | Spatial extent in decimal degrees. Use a tool like [bboxfinder.com](http://bboxfinder.com) (copy the **Box** values). |
   | **`DATETIME`** | `start/end` | Temporal extent. Use `YYYY-MM-DD` format (e.g., `2020-01-01/2020-12-31`). |
: `make setup` parameters {#tbl-setup-params}

::: {.callout-tip}
## Note
These parameters can also be used individually with `make index` to add more data later without running a full setup (see [Target reference](operations.qmd#target-reference) Table).
:::
